claim: Gemini can ingest 9+ hours of audio (1920 tokens per minute) and roughly 1 hour of video due to 1M token limit; configure to analyze specific timestamp ranges for longer content
actionable: Use Gemini for multimodal document understanding; ingest long audio/video by specifying timestamp ranges or use context caching to reduce costs by 90%
source_title: Any-to-Any: Building Native Multimodal Agents - Patrick Löber, Google DeepMind

claim: Gemini's native generation models (Nano Banana for images, text-to-speech models) understand world knowledge from base model, enabling context-aware generation (e.g., draw arrows on map -> generate Golden Gate Bridge)
actionable: Use native Gemini generation models as agentic tools; they inherit world understanding from base model, enabling smarter generation beyond simple prompt matching
source_title: Any-to-Any: Building Native Multimodal Agents - Patrick Löber, Google DeepMind

claim: Building multimodal agents requires agentic loop where reasoning model (Gemini 3) decides what generation tools to invoke (image, speech) based on task analysis
actionable: Structure multimodal agents with reasoning model deciding tool calls to specialized generation models; use function calling to route between text/image/audio generation
source_title: Any-to-Any: Building Native Multimodal Agents - Patrick Löber, Google DeepMind

claim: Gemini's text-to-speech model supports multilingual output with configurable accents and tone; speech generation uses single architecture (audio-in, audio-out) for natural interactions
actionable: Use Gemini speech generation for multilingual agents needing natural-sounding voice output; leverage single audio architecture for real-time interactions
source_title: Any-to-Any: Building Native Multimodal Agents - Patrick Löber, Google DeepMind

claim: Context caching built into Gemini API saves 90% of costs when making repeated queries on same long documents, enabling cost-effective long-context applications
actionable: Enable context caching when requerying same documents; 90% cost savings on cached tokens makes long-context workflows economically viable
source_title: Any-to-Any: Building Native Multimodal Agents - Patrick Löber, Google DeepMind

claim: Reasoning agents can decide what to generate rather than hardcoded workflows; use function calling to invoke generation based on analyzed content
actionable: Replace fixed generation pipelines with agentic loops where reasoning model analyzes content and decides what outputs (text, image, audio) to generate
source_title: Any-to-Any: Building Native Multimodal Agents - Patrick Löber, Google DeepMind
